name: Group
summary: A Group class contains methods for manipulating groups of entities. Groups in SketchUp are very similar to components, but can from a user point of view be thought of as unique objects. Groups can be instanced when copied but are silently made unique when edited through the GUI. To honor this behavior, make sure to call #make_unique before modifying a group through the API.

methods.name: add_observer
methods.summary: The add_observer method is used to add a ComponentInstance observer to the group.
methods.remarks: The add_observer method is used to add a ComponentInstance observer to the group.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: copy
methods.summary: The copy method is used to create a new Group object that is a copy of the group.
methods.remarks: The copy method is used to create a new Group object that is a copy of the group.

methods.name: definition
methods.summary: The definition method is used to retrieve the component definition for this group.
methods.remarks: The definition method is used to retrieve the component definition for this group.

methods.name: entities
methods.summary: The entities method is used to retrieve a collection of entities in the group.
methods.remarks: The entities method is used to retrieve a collection of entities in the group.

methods.name: equals?
methods.summary: The equals? method is used to determine if a group is geometrically equivalent to another group.
methods.remarks: The equals? method is used to determine if a group is geometrically equivalent to another group.

params.name: group
params.doc: The group to compare this group with.

methods.name: explode
methods.summary: The explode method is used to explode the group into individual entities.
methods.remarks: The explode method is used to explode the group into individual entities.

returns.doc: An array of entity objects if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: guid
methods.summary: The guid method is used to get the base 64 encoded unique id for this SketchUp object.
methods.remarks: The guid method is used to get the base 64 encoded unique id for this SketchUp object.

methods.name: intersect
methods.summary: Note: This method is not available in SketchUp Make.
methods.remarks: Note: This method is not available in SketchUp Make. The intersect method is used to compute the boolean intersection of two groups representing manifold solid volumes (this & arg). If the specified objects (this and arg) do not represent manifold volumes, this method fails.

params.name: group
params.doc: The group to intersect this group with.

returns.doc: The resultant group if the two objects (this and arg) represent manifold solids and the operation succeeds. Otherwise nil is returned.

methods.name: local_bounds
methods.summary: Deprecated.
methods.remarks: Deprecated. In favor of `group.definition.bounds`. The #local_bounds method is used to retrieve the Geom::BoundingBox bounding the contents of a Sketchup::Group, in the group's own internal coordinate system.

methods.name: locked?
methods.summary: The locked? method is used to determine if a group is locked.
methods.remarks: The locked? method is used to determine if a group is locked.

methods.name: make_unique
methods.summary: The #make_unique method is used to force a group to have a unique definition.
methods.remarks: The #make_unique method is used to force a group to have a unique definition. If the group is already unique in the model, nothing happens. Copying a group in SketchUp will create a group that shares the same definition. SketchUp implicitly makes group unique when edited from the GUI, and from a user point of view groups could be thought of as always being unique. To honor this behavior, call this method before editing a group through the API.

methods.name: manifold?
methods.summary: The manifold? method is used to determine if a group is manifold.
methods.remarks: The manifold? method is used to determine if a group is manifold.

methods.name: move!
methods.summary: Note: Despite the name being similar to #transform!, this method closer corresponds to #transformation=.
methods.remarks: Note: Despite the name being similar to #transform!, this method closer corresponds to #transformation=. The #move! method is used to set the transformation of this group instance, similarly to #transformation= but without recording to the undo stack. This method is useful for moving entities inside of an animation or page transition.

returns.doc: the transformed Group object if successful

methods.name: outer_shell
methods.summary: The outer_shell method is used to compute the outer shell of the two groups representing manifold solid volumes (this || arg).
methods.remarks: The outer_shell method is used to compute the outer shell of the two groups representing manifold solid volumes (this || arg). If the specified objects (this and arg) do not represent manifold volumes, this method fails.

params.name: group
params.doc: The group to outer shell this group with.

returns.doc: The resultant group if the two objects (this and arg) represent manifold solids and the operation succeeds otherwise nil is returned.

methods.name: remove_observer
methods.summary: The remove_observer method is used to remove a ComponentInstance observer from the group.
methods.remarks: The remove_observer method is used to remove a ComponentInstance observer from the group.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: show_differences
methods.summary: The show_differences method is used to determine if a group is geometrically equivalent to another group and in addition move the non- matching and matching geometry to new layers.
methods.remarks: The show_differences method is used to determine if a group is geometrically equivalent to another group and in addition move the non- matching and matching geometry to new layers. This method will move both groups to Layer0. Geometry that is the same in both groups will be moved to a new layer named group_name + “_same”. Geometry that is not the same will be moved to a layer named group_name + “_diff”. If verbose is true, a list of all the geometry that is different from one group to the other is displayed texturally in the Ruby Console.

params.name: group
params.doc: The group to be compared with.

params.name: verbose
params.doc: A boolean flag indicating whether to display a textural report of the found differences to the Ruby console.

returns.doc: true if the groups are geometrically equivalent. Otherwise false.

methods.name: split
methods.summary: Note: This method is not available in SketchUp Make.
methods.remarks: Note: This method is not available in SketchUp Make. The split method is used to compute the boolean split (map overlay) of the two groups representing manifold solid volumes (this ^ arg). If the specified objects (this and arg) do not represent manifold volumes, this method fails.

params.name: group
params.doc: The group to split this group with.

returns.doc: A vector (array) of the three resultant groups If the two objects (this and arg) represent manifold solids and the operation succeeds otherwise nil is returned. The 3 groups are as follows: The intersection of volume 1 & volume 2, the difference of volume 1 minus volume 2, and the reverse difference of volume 2 minus volume 1.

methods.name: subtract
methods.summary: Note: This method is not available in SketchUp Make.
methods.remarks: Note: This method is not available in SketchUp Make. The subtract method is used to compute the boolean difference of the two groups representing manifold solid volumes (this - arg). If the specified objects (this and arg) do not represent manifold volumes, this method fails.

params.name: group
params.doc: The group to subtract this group from.

returns.doc: The resultant group if the two objects (this and arg) represent manifold solids and the operation succeeds. Otherwise nil is returned.

methods.name: to_component
methods.summary: The to_component method is used to convert the group to a component instance.
methods.remarks: The to_component method is used to convert the group to a component instance.

methods.name: transform!
methods.summary: The transform! method is used to apply a transformation to a group.
methods.remarks: The transform! method is used to apply a transformation to a group.

returns.doc: a transformed group object if successful

methods.name: trim
methods.summary: Note: Trimming object group2 using group1 results in a new trimmed version of group2.
methods.remarks: Note: Trimming object group2 using group1 results in a new trimmed version of group2. If the trim is successful the original group2 is erased and a newly trimmed version is created. This new version, derived from the trimming operation, will possess a new GUID and will be returned. Note: This method is not available in SketchUp Make. The #trim method is used to compute the (non-destructive) boolean difference of the two groups representing manifold solid volumes (this - arg). If the specified objects (this and arg) do not represent manifold volumes, this method fails.

params.name: group
params.doc: The group to trim this group against.

returns.doc: The resultant group if the two objects (this and arg) represent manifold solids and the operation succeeds otherwise nil is returned.

methods.name: union
methods.summary: Note: This method is not available in SketchUp Make.
methods.remarks: Note: This method is not available in SketchUp Make. The union method is used to compute the boolean union of the two groups representing manifold solid volumes (this | arg). If the specified objects (this and arg) do not represent manifold volumes, this method fails.

params.name: group
params.doc: The group to union this group with.

returns.doc: The resultant group if the two objects (this and arg) represent manifold solids and the operation succeeds. Otherwise nil is returned.

methods.name: volume
methods.summary: The volume method is used to compute the volume of this group if and only if this group is manifold.
methods.remarks: The volume method is used to compute the volume of this group if and only if this group is manifold.

returns.doc: If the group represents a manifold volume, volume will be a positive value. If volume is negative, the group is not manifold.

properties.name: description
properties.summary: The description method is used to retrieve the description for the group.
properties.remarks: The description method is used to retrieve the description for the group.

properties.name: glued_to
properties.summary: The #glued_to method is used to retrieve the entity that this group is glued to.
properties.remarks: The #glued_to method is used to retrieve the entity that this group is glued to.

properties.name: locked
properties.summary: The locked= method is used to lock a group.
properties.remarks: The locked= method is used to lock a group.

properties.name: name
properties.summary: The name method is used to retrieve the name of the group.
properties.remarks: The name method is used to retrieve the name of the group.

properties.name: transformation
properties.summary: The transformation method is used to retrieve the transformation for the group.
properties.remarks: The transformation method is used to retrieve the transformation for the group.
